dialogos.texto_en: Huh? Futaro-kun, are you alone?
Oh, it's Ichika.
What now?\nYou seem a bit disappointed.
No, not really.
Hmm. Well, let's just leave it at that.
This is the Native Okinawan Village, right?\nIt's surprising to see you taking an interest\nin a place like this.
Really?\nSince I came all the way here, I wanted to see buildings\nthat truly scream Okinawa!
Oh, Uesugi-san?!
Ah!\nY-Yotsuba, you were here too, huh.
What a coincidence to meet here like this.
Yeah, it is. I just happened to come this way.
R-right.
Hmmm...
Yotsuba.
Hm? What?
If you want to look around with Futaro-kun,\nwhy don't you invite him instead of hesitating?
Whaaaat?!
Huh? What's the matter?
Nothing!\nWe're just talking about something else!
I-Ichika!\nThe timing now's a little off...
Hey, Futaro-kun.\nHow about taking a look around with Yotsuba?
Uwawaa?!\nB-but I'm looking around at things\nwith you right now, Ichika!
Don't worry about me,\nyou're free to go wherever you want.
Umm, well, uh...
L-look!\nEven you must want to see something, right?\nPlease go ahead, don't mind me!
Well, it is free time, isn't it.
That's right.\nIt's your special free time, isn't it?
Yeah.\nI get what you're saying.
Hmm, this is tough.
Ah, look at that!\nThere's a shisa on the roof! See?!
Y-yeah, you're right!\nWhy is it placed up there?
Come on...
It's impossible to just ignore Ichika.\nWe'd just be leaving her all alone.
Th-that's right!\nThat'd be so selfish of us!
Even still it's ok to be a little selfish sometimes.
Huh? Ichika?
Both of you, Futaro-kun and Yotsuba,\nare serious, in a good way AND bad way.
Ah, look at that!\nThere's a shisa on the roof! See?!
Y-yeah, you're right!\nWhy is it placed up there?
Come on...
It might be nice to go see things together.
Huh?
...Ummm.
Heh, it seems like your true feelings\nslipped out accidentally, huh?
So? What are you gonna do?
Well, even Yotsuba must have things she wants to see,\nand since it's free time, I think we should prioritize\nwhat each person wants to see, right?
Th-that's right!\nIt IS free time, after all.
I think it's okay for you to decide\nwhat you want to prioritize.
Ah, look at that!\nThere's a shisa on the roof! See?!
Y-yeah, you're right!\nWhy is it placed up there?
Come on...
Yeah, yeah, it's a shisa.
They say it's protecting the 'Hinukan,'\nwhich is the god of the hearth in that house.
Oh, really.\nYou're quite knowledgeable.
Actually, I just heard it earlier.\nIt's what the guide told me.
Huh? But, isn't it strange to put it on the roof\nto protect the god of the hearth?
Most old houses used to have thatched roofs,\nso it's to prevent fires from breaking out.
Wow, I see.
Makes sense.
Speaking of which, we often see shisa in the city\nwith their mouths open or closed, right?
Probably the A-un pair, yeah?
A-un...\nAre those the komainu at shrines?
Oh, that's right.\nThe A-un pair of komainu\nsymbolizes the beginning and the end of all things.
Wasn't that explained by the guide?
Well, I didn't hear it directly from the guide.\nI just overheard it\nwhen they were explaining to someone else.
In that case, it's time to ask for help\nfrom the amazing smartphone!
Let me see...
Shisa come in male and female pairs,\nwith one with an open mouth being male,\nand the one with a closed mouth being female.
Oh, I didn't know that they were male and female.
The male protects the house from external threats,\nwhile the female prevents good fortune\nfrom escaping outside, or so they say.
I see.\nThat's why they're often placed in front of gates.
They always stick together to protect the house,\ndon't they?
In that case,\nwhy don't you two go be lovey-dovey too?
HUH?!
Don't mind me, just go have fun together,\nyou two lovebirds.
I said, stop worrying about us like that.
That's right! Since we're on a trip together,\nwe should all have fun together!
Come on, Ichika! Let's go see the next place!
Aa-ah, really, now.\nWell then, see you later, Futaro-kun.
